page: Amazon Web Services a1.xlarge is an ARM64-based server powered by the first-generation AWS Graviton processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.3 GHz on the Cortex-A72 microarchitecture. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the a1.xlarge delivers modest processing capabilities. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance is positioned in the average tier, while multi-core performance falls into the weak category. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both read and write operations are in the bottom decile, with uncached large-block read speeds recorded at 12.56 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is supported by hardware-accelerated AES, but the processor lacks advanced vector extensions such as AVX or AMX.

The primary tradeoff of this server is its lower resource density and legacy ARMv8 architecture, which results in lower performance in memory-intensive and modern vector-aligned workloads. However, it offers a cost-efficient entry point for ARM64 development, basic web serving, and lightweight containerized applications. It is best suited for static web serving, basic database operations, and software compilation tasks that do not require high memory throughput or advanced instruction sets.
description: Amazon Web Services a1.xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by the first-generation AWS Graviton Cortex-A72 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of memory, a 1 Gbps baseline network speed, and no local storage, operating under the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU performance but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results, with uncached large-block reads at 12.56 GB/sec. Lacking AVX and AMX extensions, it relies on hardware AES for cryptography. This server represents a low-cost entry point for ARM64 development, making it suitable for lightweight workloads such as static web serving, basic database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services a1.xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 1 Gbps network interface, designed for entry-level ARM development and basic web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services a1.xlarge is an ARM64 server with 4 dedicated Graviton cores and 8.0 GB of RAM, suited for basic web serving and ARM development.
tagline: An entry-level ARM64 compute instance featuring dedicated physical cores for lightweight scale-out workloads and basic development environments.
bullet_points: First-generation AWS Graviton ARM64 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
8.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Nitro hypervisor integration with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps
Hardware-accelerated AES cryptography without AVX or AMX vector extensions
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and ARM64 development
